text: Open Settings to configure the app, permissions and language.
Settings groups app-wide options: the permissions the app needs, the interface language, and analytics consent. Nothing here starts or stops the server.
Show a QR code so a nearby device can join and open your library.
The QR encodes the Wi-Fi network (or hotspot) plus the address of your library. Guests scan it to connect and browse — no typing required.
Switch between light and dark theme.
The installed app version.
Share your library over the current Wi-Fi network.
When your phone is connected to Wi-Fi, other devices on the same network can open your library at your phone's address.
Turn on a hotspot so nearby devices can connect with no external network.
The hotspot creates a local Wi-Fi network from your phone. It works even with no SIM or internet — ideal for sharing offline.
Open your library in the browser.
Start or stop the content server.
Other devices can only reach your library while the server is running. The first start can take a few seconds.
Advanced network options.
Enable custom DNS settings for the server.
Only needed in special network setups; leave it off unless you know you need it.
Apply the DNS settings you entered.
Show the connection log.
Clear the connection log.
Copy the whole log to the clipboard.
